0001: Fewer than 12 points and not suitable for a weak opening: <red>Pass</red>.
0002: Balanced hand (4333, 4432 or 5332) with 12 to 14 points: <red>1NT</red>.
0003: Balanced or semi-balanced hand with 20 to 22 points: <red>2NT</red>.
0004: Unbalanced hand, 12 to 22 points or balanced hand with 15 to 19 points: <red>1 of longest suit</red>.
0005: Very strong hand with 23 points or more, or hand that you wish to force to game, 2!c.
0006:
With 12 to 19 points and two 5 card suits: 1 of higher ranking suit.

(If the 5 card suits are !s and !c, bid 1!c).
0008: Acol light opener with a 6 card suit and 11 points: <red>1 of longest suit</red>.
0010: With an unbalanced hand: bid to game in longest suit.
0011:
Weak with 6 to 10 points and a 6 card suit: 2 of that suit.

(With 6-6 (rare), the higher ranking suit.)
0012: A 7 card suit and 0-9 points,  <red>3 of long suit</red> (preemptive).
0013: Fewer than 11 points, not suitable for weak take-out: <red>Pass</red>.
0014: With a 5+ card major: bid long suit at the next level..
0016: 19+ points and a six card major: <red>4 of major</red>.
0017: 11+ points and 4 card major: 2♣. (Stayman)
0021: 4+ points and 4+ card major: 3♣. (Stayman)
0023: No support for partner, but 12+ points: <red>DOUBLE</red>.
0026: With support from partner and 19+ points: <red>bid to game in suit</red>.
0027: Fewer than 16 points and no support for partner: <red>Pass</red>
0030: Support for partner and 16+ points: <red>jump in partner's suit</red>.
0033: Fewer than 8 points: 2♦ (negative).
0035: Fewer than 10 points and 2+ card support: <red>4 of suit</red> (preemptive raise).
0036: With a 7 card suit and 8+ points: <red>2 of longest suit</red>.
0037: Fewer than 15 points and no support: <red>Pass</red>
0040: With 12 points or a  poor hand with 13 points: <red>Pass</red>
0044: Response to Stayman, with no 4 card major: <red>2!d</red>.
0045: Partner has bid game. No slam interest: <red>Pass</red>
0046: Support for partner's major: <red>4 of major</red>.
0047: With fewer than 3 card support for partner's major: <red>3NT</red>
0048: With an unbalanced hand: <red>second suit at appropriate level</red>.
0049: Support for partner and 13+ points (include shortage and/or length points): bid to game.
0053:
With no support for partner or partner has opened with a minor, 9+ points: 1 (or 2 if necessary) of longest suit.

(NB 1♠ followed by 2♥ usually promises 5+♥s .)
0054: With 3+ card support for partner's major: <red>raise partner's suit</red>.
0055: Partner has passed and unable to compete: <red>Pass</red>
0057: Support for partner and a very strong hand: <red>bid to game</red>.
0059: Partner has shown preference for your long suit: bid game in long suit.
0060: With no support for partner and a 4+ card major biddable at the 1 level: 1 of suit.
0062: With 15+ points and a balanced hand: <red>3NT</red>.
0063: With 15+ points and 2+ card support for partner's major: 4 of major.
0066: Partner has indicate a strong hand and with stoppers in bid suits: <red>3NT</red>.
0067: Minimum hand or no 5+ card suit. Cannot show second suit: <red>Pass</red>.
0069: Partnre has jumped in your suit, with fewer than 14 points in a major or 16 points in a minor: <red>Pass</red>.
0070: 5 card support for partner's minor and 16+ points: <red>6 of minor</red>.
0071: Partner has jumped in your suit. With 14+ points in a major or 16+ points in a minor: <red>bid game in suit</red>.
0073: With no support for partner and 10 points or fewer: Pass.
0074: No biddable second suit and 3 card support for partner: <red>raise in partner's suit</red>.
0075:
Partner has broken the barrier showing 16+ points and a 5/4 hand. With 8 points or fewer,
  • if suit preference is a minor: bid at lowest level
  • if suit preference is a major: bid to game
  • with no support: 3NT
.
0076: Partner has shown a weak 5/4 hand: show suit preference.
0077: Partner has invited game. With 8 or 9 points: 3NT.
0078: Partner has invited game. With 7 points or fewer: Pass.
0079:
Partner has broken the barrier showing 16+ points and a 5/4 hand. With 9+ points: bid to game.
  • support partner's suit if possible, or
  • 3NT with stoppers in unbid suits, or
  • own 7 card major.
0080:
Partner has shown a 5/4 hand. With 13+ points: bid game:
  • support partner's suit if possible, or
  • 3NT with stoppers in unbid suits, or
  • own 7 card major.
0081: With a strong hand, a 6+ card suit and no support for partner: rebid long suit.
0082: 13+ points and support for partner's minor: <red>Double</red>.
0083: Partner has shown 15 to 18 points and a balanced hand. With 10+ points: 3NT
0084: Partner has shown 15 to 16 points and a balanced hand. With 9 to 12 points: 2NT
0085: Partner has shown 15 to 16 points and a balanced hand. With 8 points or fewer: Pass.
0086: With 8+ points and a balanced hand: 2NT.
0087: With 16+ points and support for partner's major: 4NT. (Blackwood)
0088: With support from partner and 19+points: 6 of agreed suit.
0089: With a 6 card suit and 16+ points: jump in long suit.
0090: With an 8 card suit: 4 of long siut.
0091: With a good 5+ card suit: 1 of long suit.
0092: With a good 5+ card suit: 1 of long suit.
0093: With a good 5+ card suit and 11+ points: bid suit at the next level.
0094: With no biddable suit at this level and tolerance for all unbid suits: Double.
0096: With a 4441 hand: <red>1 of suit below singleton</red> (if singleton is !c, then 1<red>!h</red>).
0098: Fewer than 15 points: <red>bid lowest ranking suit at lowest level</red>.
0099: With a good long suit and an opening hand: bid suit at the next level.
0100: With a good long suit and an opening hand: bid suit at the next level.
0102: With good support for partner and a distributional hand: bid game in partner's suit.
0104: Partner has shown 11 or 12  points and a balanced hand: <red>3NT</red>
0105: No support for partner. With 11+ points and 5/4 hand (or better): show second suit.
0106:
With no support and a balanced hand (or shortage in partner's suit):
  • with 9 to 13 points: 2NT,
  • with 14+ points: 3NT.
0107: With no support for partner: rebid own long suit at the appropriate level.
0108: With 10+ points and a 7 card suit: bid game in long suit.
0109: Partner has indicated a very strong hand and you have support: <red>4NT</red> (Blackwood).
0110: With 8 points or fewer, or 9 points and only a 4 card suit: Pass.
0112: With 5 card support for partner: bid partner's suit at the next level.
0113: With a powerful long suit:bid suit at the next level.
0114: With 3+ card support for partner and 13+ points: bid partners' suit at eh 4 level.
0115: With no stops in bid suits: Pass.
0116: With a balanced hand, stops in bid suits and 8+ points: 1NT.
0117: With a balanced hand, stops in bid suits and 14+ points: 3NT.
0118: Partner has responded at the lowest level. With 15 points or fewer: <red>Pass</red>.
0119: With 10+ points and a (semi) balanced hand: 3NT.
0120: With some support for partner; Bid game in partner's suit.
0121:
Partner has support for a 4 card major:
  • with 11 or 12 points: 3 of major
  • with 13+ points: 4 of major.
.
0122: With support for partner's suit and a better than minimum hand: bid partner's suit at the next level.
0123: With a two suited hand and a better than minimum hand: bid second suit at the next level.
0124: With no support for partner, stops in bid suits and 8+ points: 2NT.
0125: No support from partner, an overcall and no six card suit: <red>Pass</red>.
0126: With an 8 card major and 8+ points: bid to game in major.
0128: With no support from partner: Pass.
0129: Show strength in agreed suit: bid suit at the next level.
0130: Partner has responded at the lowest level. With 16 to 18 points: <red> bid suit at the 3  level</red>.
0131: With 4+ card support for partner and a strong hand: bid to game in partner's suit.
0132: With fewer than 4 card support for partner: bid long suit at the next level.
0136: With 16+ points , a 6+ card suit, and fewer than 2 cards in partner's suit: 2 of own suit.
0137: Acol light opener with a 6 card suit and 11 points: 1 of longest suit
0138: Overcaller has doubled showing 16+ points and advancer has intervened: <red>Pass</red>.
0141: No support for partner, and fewer than 12 points: <red>Pass</red>.
0143: Partner has made an invitational bid in your suit: <red>bid suit at the 4 level</red>.
0144: With a 7 card major: bid to game in the major.
0145: Partner is weak. With 25+ points and a semi-balanced hand: <red>3NT</red>.
0146: Partner has shown 13+ points and a balanced hand. With 18+ points explore slam: <red>4NT</red> (Blackwood).
0147: Semi-balanced and partner has indicated game values: <red>3NT</red>.
0148: With no suit agreement and a strong hand: bid to game in best suit.
0149: With a long minor and 4+ cards in opponents suit: Pass.
0150: With support for partner's suit: jump in agreed suit.
0151: With an exceptionally long suit: bid 4 of suit.
0152: With 6+ card suit and up to 15 points: <red>rebid suit</red>.
0153: With an 8 card suit, fewer than 15 points and no second suit: <red>bid to game in suit</red>.
0155:
Partner has jumped in a minor.

With 4 card support: bid suit at the next level.
0156: With a 8+ card major: bid long suit at the next level.
0157: With 5+ cards in second suit: bid second suit at the next level.
0158: With 4 aces: bid agreed suit at the 6 level.
0159: Partner has some values. With a long, strong suit: bid suit at the next level.
0160: Partner has shown minimum support: Pass.
0161: Partner has shown a long suit. With a (semi) balanced hand and stops in bid suits: 3NT.
0162: With 15+ points and a good 5+ card suit: bid long suit at the next level.
0163: With 5/5 hand and 16+ points: <red> second suit at the next level</red>.
0165: With support for partner's suit: <red>raise partner's suit</red>.
0166: With a minimum hand and no suit agreement: Pass.
0167: Partner has shown game values. With a six card major: <red>bid game in major</red>.
0168: With 5/4 in the majors: bid second major at the next level.
0169: With a (semi) balanced better than minimum hand: 3NT.
0170: With 3+ card support for partner: 6 of partner's suit.
0171: Partner has bid to game: Pass.
0172: With a 5/4 hand: bid second suit at the next level.
0173: With a 5/5 hand: bid second suit at the next level.
0174: With a strong hand and a 5+card suit: bid suit at the next level.
0175: With two five card suits and 16+ points: <red>jump in second suit</red>.
0176: With no support for partner: 3NT.
0177: With a strong hand and a biddable suit: Double. (Take out)
0178: With a 5/5 hand: bid second suit (if possible).

With no support and a 5/4 hand:
  • with 9 to 12 points: 2NT,
  • with 13+ points: 3NT.

With no support for partner and a distributional hand:

With 2+ card support for partner: raise partner's suit.

(With 12 points, bid at the 4 level).

0206: With a (semi) balanced hand and stops in bid suits: 2NT.
0207: Bid best long suit at the appropriate level.

With 6/4 hand: rebid long suit , or, with a good 4 card suit: bid that suit.

(With 19+ points, jump).

0229: With a (semi) balanced hand and 13+ points: 3NT.

2NT.
